title: uapaca littoralis
intro: Uapaca littoralis, also known as the African breadfruit, is a tropical plant species native to the coastal regions of West Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory problems, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
